headlines.title: Israeli strike kills journalists in Lebanon, as Yemen says Iran 'dragging country into war'
Iran-backed Houthis join war with attack against Israel
Shops and restaurants in Egypt told to close early as energy crisis deepens
Twenty-two migrants die off Greek coast after six days at sea
Anti-Trump rallies in thousands of US cities for 'No Kings' protest
WTO members agree on first baseline for digital trade rules
Tens of thousands protest in London against far-right Reform UK party
Yemen's Houthis claim responsibility for missile attack on Israel as war in Middle East intensifies
Russia is sending upgraded drones used in the Ukraine war to Iran
Rubio denies Zelenskyy's claim that the US asked Ukraine to cede land to Russia
At least 15 US troops wounded in Iran strike on Saudi airbase
Iran warns neighbours not to let 'enemies run the war' from their land
Russian strikes on Ukraine kill 4 as deal to end war dims
Nepal's ex-PM Oli detained over alleged role in deadly protest crackdown

summary: Major world news on March 28, 2026 is dominated by escalating tensions in the Middle East, with Iran-backed Houthi rebels from Yemen joining the conflict by launching missile attacks on Israel. At least 15 US troops were wounded in an Iranian strike on a Saudi airbase. Meanwhile, thousands participated in 'No Kings' anti-Trump protests across US cities, and tens of thousands marched in London against the far-right Reform UK party. Other notable stories include 22 migrants dying off the Greek coast, Egypt implementing energy crisis measures, and Nepal's former PM being detained.
